sourceMovie.title: Bronson
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Bronson for the same reason it broke through in 2009: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a action rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Nicolas Winding Refn keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Lock Up
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Bronson clicked for you, Lock Up (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Shawshank Redemption
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Bronson clicked for you, The Shawshank Redemption (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Bronson?
faq.answer: Try Lock Up, The Shawshank Redemption, Extremely Wicked, Shockingly Evil and Vile—each shares DNA with Bronson while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Bronson?
faq.answer: Bronson (2009) is best described as Drama, Action, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Bronson worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
